title: New people vs new world
summary:
[]: New people vs. new world             Quote Post by Ah Q » Wed Aug 18, 2021 5:53 pm I noticed a discrepancy between the English subtitles and English dub of 3.0+1.0. During the "Neon Genesis" scene, Shinji says the following in the English dub: I won't rewind time or revert the world. I'm just going to rewrite the world into one that doesn't have Evas. A world where new people can live.

[]: Re: New people vs. new world  &

chunks.content:
Article: New people vs new world
[]: New people vs. new world             Quote Post by Ah Q » Wed Aug 18, 2021 5:53 pm I noticed a discrepancy between the English subtitles and English dub of 3.0+1.0. During the "Neon Genesis" scene, Shinji says the following in the English dub: I won't rewind time or revert the world. I'm just going to rewrite the world into one that doesn't have Evas. A world where new people can live.

[]: Re: New people vs. new world             Quote Post by Raikyu » Wed Aug 18, 2021 6:10 pm I remember when the script leaked in March, and the translations at the time said "new world".

[]: Re: New people vs. new world             Quote Post by aboose » Wed Aug 18, 2021 7:59 pm Sure, I'll check it for you. 時間も世界も戻さない I won't turn back time or the world. ただ, エヴァがなくてもいい世界に書き換えるだけだ I will just rewrite the world to be okay without Evangelion 新しい人が生きていける世界に A new world where people can go on to live 世界の新たな創生 Rei: The creation of a new world ネオンジェネシス Rei: Neon Genesis. The reason the person originally mistranslated it is because in Japanese, the word order is different than English. The literal translation of this line: 新しい人が生きていける世界に "New people can go on living in world to" At first glance it can look like the modifier "new" is applied to "people", but it's actually applied to "world" as you would use a comma in English: "New, people can go on living in, world to" This is highlighted by Ayanami's next line highlighting that it's the creation of a new world, not a new people.

[]: Re: New people vs. new world             Quote Post by Kendrix » Wed Aug 18, 2021 8:08 pm Ah! So it simply means that the new world is going to be "human habitable"

[]: Re:

Article: New people vs new world
 vs. new world             Quote Post by Kendrix » Wed Aug 18, 2021 8:08 pm Ah! So it simply means that the new world is going to be "human habitable"

[]: Re: New people vs. new world             Quote Post by aboose » Wed Aug 18, 2021 8:13 pm I think it's safe to say the Dub is not canon due to all the mistakes it has. I'm obviously biased because I don't like the dub, but even with English subtitles miss some nuances that I think dedicated fan subbers would have caught. The dub is just flat out wrong in many places.

[]: Re: New people vs. new world             Quote Post by Registration2 » Wed Aug 18, 2021 8:36 pm It is funny even comparing other languages how different the result is, sometimes it says recreate, sometimes rewrite, and even a new creation, even in the same language from different places.

[]: Re: New people vs. new world             Quote Post by NightComesOn » Fri Aug 20, 2021 9:41 am The biggest revelation for me here is that Shinji isn't just creating a "world that doesn't have Evas." He's creating a world that will be "okay without Evangelion." That seems to me like a major change of meaning. He's not getting rid of Evas; he's getting rid of the NEED for Evas. In a diegetic sense I suppose that means a world without the FAR and the seeds and the angels--where Eva is necessary. In a meta sense, I think it means "You can move on from Evangelion now. We as a world have moved beyond the need for Evangelion. Time to go up the stairs into the real world."

[]: Re: New people vs. new world             Quote Post by Natalie the Cat » Fri Aug 20, 2021 12:08 pm NightComesOn wrote: The biggest revelation for me here is that Shinji isn't just creating a "world that doesn't have Evas." He's creating a world that will be "okay without Evangelion." That seems to me like a major change of meaning. He's not getting rid of Evas; he's getting rid of the NEED for Evas. In a diegetic sense I suppose that means a world without the FAR and the seeds and the angels

Article: New people vs new world
 seems to me like a major change of meaning. He's not getting rid of Evas; he's getting rid of the NEED for Evas. In a diegetic sense I suppose that means a world without the FAR and the seeds and the angels--where Eva is necessary. In a meta sense, I think it means "You can move on from Evangelion now. We as a world have moved beyond the need for Evangelion. Time to go up the stairs into the real world."
